title: Fix elsif/else evaluation: parsed as separate statements not continuation
description:
The form parser terminates an if-statement at the closing '}' brace (read_stmt returns when it sees LBrace/Block). This means 'elsif' and 'else' keywords become separate top-level statements, not part of the if-statement form.

eval_elsif_else() in evaluator.rs is effectively dead code: 'after_block' slice in eval_if() is always empty because the block terminates the statement. The elsif/else continuations are never evaluated.

Symptom: in a script 'if COND1 { A; } elsif COND2 { B; } else { C; }', when COND1 is false, the result is always Keep (implicit) rather than evaluating COND2 or the else branch.

Note: test_elsif_else_branch_taken passes only accidentally — the else block contains 'keep;' which produces the same result as the implicit keep that fires when no branch executes.

Failing test: test_elsif_second_branch_taken in tests/rfc_vectors.rs expects FileInto('Lists') when the elsif condition matches, but gets Keep.

Fix needed in evaluator.rs: the script-level eval_stmt_list must detect consecutive elsif/else statements following an if-result and chain them correctly. The evaluator needs to carry forward the 'branch taken' state through the statement list, so that when 'if' returns Continue (branch not taken), subsequent elsif/else statements in the same script-level sequence are processed rather than skipped.
